title: February 2024 Back Page – Tariff Rate Changes and Annual Meetings
summary: For the last two years AVEC has been operating at a loss. summary: The Petersburg Borough Assembly has a long agenda ahead of their regular meeting today at 12 p.m. They’re scheduled to look at two ordinances that would change parts of the Petersburg Municipal Code that dictate how they can sell or exchange Borough property. title: Where to catch races around Alaska
summary: The Iditarod is Alaska’s most famous sled dog race, but it’s not the only one. Here are some other races happening this winter. Yukon Quest The Yukon Quest, begins February 3. Formerly, a 1,000-mile odyssey between Fairbanks and Whitehorse, Yukon, this year’s three races range from 100 to 450 miles,...

title: February in Alaska History
summary: Eielson’s epic mail flight On February 21, 1924, Carl Ben Eielson flew Alaska’s first official air mail service. The 280-mile flight from Fairbanks to McGrath took just a few hours, compared to 18 days or more by dog sled. Eielson used a de Havilland DH-4 open cockpit biplane to carry the 164 pounds...

title: In their own words
summary: Oral history on the upper Tanana Dene released The Upper Tanana Dene, People of this Land (University of Alaska Press), offers a portrait of an Alaska Native people both before and during the transformative changes of the 20th century. title: Arctic Winter Games Return to Alaska
summary: March events bring sports and culture After 10 years abroad, the Arctic Winter Games return to Alaska this March 10-16. title: Names on the Land
summary: Alaska Native place names in this issue Iilgayaq (Bristol Bay) Possibly refers to a place to hide. Some Yup’ik language speakers say the name describes all of Bristol Bay, while others say it only refers to the Nushagak area of the bay.
